objective: Define and validate a repo-local SQL schema drift probe contract for destructive maintenance surfaces.
scope_boundary: Add governance-owned SQL schema drift probe contract documentation and example fixtures.
Add a deterministic validator plus focused tests for the contract.
Wire hldpro-governance Local CI to run the contract tests when SQL drift contract surfaces change.
Record governance evidence, registries, progress, fail-fast, and self-learning updates for issue #534.
out_of_scope: Creating a broad global SQL abstraction or migration runner.
research_summary: Issue #534 remains open under parent #533. Issue #535 added the SQL schema drift session-error KB entry but explicitly did not implement schema drift probes. The current repo has Local CI profile and validator patterns suitable for a governance-owned contract with repo-local consumer hooks. The accepted decision is repo-local Local CI profile placement: governance validates the contract shape, product repos own dialect-specific live probes and adoption PRs.

sprints.name: Repo-local SQL schema drift probe contract
sprints.goal: Prevent destructive SQL maintenance from relying on stale schema assumptions without a pre-mutation metadata probe.
tasks: Document the repo-local Local CI profile placement decision.
Define the v1 contract fields for metadata probes, required columns, destructive operations, fixtures, negative controls, Local CI hooks, and residual risk.
Add a HealthcarePlatform-style example for organizations.organization_id and stale org_id.
Add validator and focused negative-control tests.
Wire hldpro-governance Local CI profile coverage.
Update registries, self-learning links, progress, validation, and closeout evidence.
acceptance_criteria: The implementation decides governance standards versus consumer profiles versus repo-local Local CI profile placement.
The contract queries schema metadata rather than hardcoding stale assumptions.
A concrete example fixture and documented profile hook are present.
Destructive SQL maintenance scripts are required to run preflight probes before mutation where applicable.
The scope remains repo-specific with no broad global SQL abstraction.
Repos without SQL surfaces have an accepted residual-risk deferral path.

specialist_reviews.focus: Repo-local contract boundary, deterministic validation, and Local CI enforceability
specialist_reviews.summary: The plan keeps SQL probing repo-specific while adding governance-owned validation and a concrete stale-column negative control.

alternate_model_review.summary: Bounded governance validator/profile contract slice; no baseline STANDARDS.md change or broad global abstraction is introduced.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implementation may update the SQL schema drift probe contract, validator, focused tests, hldpro-governance Local CI profile, registries, runbooks, and required issue #534 governance evidence only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Run focused tests, governance validators, Local CI, then open and merge PR after required checks pass.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Focused SQL schema probe contract tests pass.
Contract example includes a stale-column negative control.
Local CI profile plans the SQL schema drift probe contract check for contract surface changes.
Execution remains inside the issue #534 write scope.

material_deviation_rules: If downstream product repo edits are required, stop and open repo-specific follow-up issues or PRs.
If live database access is needed, split to the owning product repo and do not run destructive SQL from governance.
If always-on Local CI exposes stale backlog mirror drift, update only the GitHub-backed mirror row required to make the gate authoritative.
If baseline STANDARDS.md changes become necessary, require the standards cross-review path before merge.
